author: śrīla rūpa gosvāmī
verses:
bhrāturantakasya pattane‛bhipatti⋅hāriṇī
prekṣayāti⋅pāpino‛pi pāpasindhu⋅tāriṇī
nīra⋅mādhurībhirapyaśeṣa⋅citta⋅bandhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

hāri⋅vāri⋅dhārayābhimaṇḍitoru⋅khāṇḍavā
puṇḍarīka⋅maṇḍalodyadaṇḍajāli⋅tāṇḍavā
snānakāma⋅pāmarogra⋅pāpasampadandhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

śīkarābhimṛṣṭa⋅jantu⋅durvipāka⋅mardinī
nanda⋅nandanāntaraṅga⋅bhaktipūra⋅vardhinī
tīra⋅saṅgamābhilāṣi⋅maṅgalānubandhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

dvīpacakravāla⋅juṣṭa⋅saptasindhu⋅bhedinī
śrīmukunda⋅nirmitoru⋅divyakeli⋅vedinī
kānti⋅kandalībhirindranīlavṛnda⋅nindinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

māthureṇa maṇḍalena cāruṇābhimaṇḍitā
premanaddha⋅vaiṣṇavādhva⋅vardhanāya paṇḍitā
ūrmi⋅dorvilāsa⋅padmanābha⋅pāda⋅vandinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

ramya⋅tīra⋅rambhamāṇa⋅gokadamba⋅bhūṣitā
divyagandhabhāk⋅kadamba⋅puṣparāji⋅rūṣitā
nandasūnu⋅bhaktasaŋgha⋅saṅgamābhinandinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

phullapakṣa⋅mallikākṣa⋅haṁsalakṣa⋅kūjitā
bhaktividdha⋅deva⋅siddha⋅kinnarāli⋅pūjitā
tīra⋅gandhavāha⋅gandha⋅janmabandha⋅randhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

cidvilāsa⋅vāripūra⋅bhūrbhuvaḥ⋅svarapinī
kīrtitāpi durmadoru⋅pāpamarma⋅tāpinī
vallavendra⋅nandanāṅgarāga⋅bhaṅga⋅gandhinī
māṁ punātu sarvadāravinda⋅bandhu⋅nandinī

tuṣṭa⋅buddhiraṣṭakena nirmalormi⋅ceṣṭitāṁ
tvāmanena bhānuputri! sarvadeva⋅veṣṭitām
jaḥ stavīti vardhayasva sarvapāpa⋅mocane
bhaktipūramasya devi! puṇḍarīka⋅locane
en_translation:
May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She removes the calamity of the city of her brother Antaka (Yama). By her sight, she delivers even the great sinner from the ocean of sin. She binds the endless heart with the sweetness of her water.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She adorned the great Khāṇḍava forest with her captivating stream of water. She has the dancing of rising flocks of birds in the circle of lotuses. She blinds the wealth of terrible sin of the wretches desiring to bathe.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She crushes the bad destiny of the living beings touched by her spray. She increases the flood of intimate devotion to the Son of Nanda. She causes auspiciousness for those desiring the contact of her bank.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She pierces the seven oceans visited by the circle of islands. She knows the great divine pastimes created by Śrī Mukunda. She mocks the multitude of sapphires with shoots of luster.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She is adorned by the beautiful circle of Mathurā. She is expert in increasing the path of the Vaiṣṇavas bound by love. She worships the feet of Padmanābha with the play of her arms of waves.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She is adorned with herds of cows mooing on her beautiful banks. She is smeared with the pollen of Kadamba flowers possessing divine fragrance. She rejoices in the contact of the group of devotees of the Son of Nanda.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She is resounded by hundreds of thousands of swans with blooming wings and jasmine eyes. She is worshipped by the gods, Siddhas, and Kinnaras pierced by devotion. She destroys the bondage of birth by the scent of the wind on her bank.

May the Daughter of the Sun, the friend of the lotus, always purify me. She is the form of Bhūr, Bhuvaḥ, and Svaḥ (the three worlds) in the flood of water of spiritual pastimes. Even when glorified, she burns the core of the terrible sin of the proud. She has the scent of the broken body-ointments of the Son of the King of Cowherds.

O Daughter of the Sun! O Devi! O Lotus-eyed one! Whoever praises you with this Octet with a satisfied mind, increase the flood of devotion of him who is active with pure waves and surrounded by all the gods, while liberating him from all sins.
